cmt:
Clear: 22'0"; Land: 6.48 AC; A partnership between Raith Capital Partners LLC and Equity Industrial Partners sold a portfolio of 33 industrial and flex properties to EQT Exeter for an undisclosed price.

The portfolio has a total RBA of over 4.5 million square feet.

Overall occupancy of the portfolio was about 90%. 

The buyer noted that all of the properties were located in markets with adjacencies to their offices. It included a mix of big box distribution and last-mile facilities with a broad spectrum of distribution functions and flexible suite sizes located in prime submarkets across the US.

The buyer intends to complete strategic make-ready improvements to fill vacancy across the assets and to work with the existing tenants to maintain occupancy and cash flow.

A press release issued by the buyer confirms the total RBA, the overall occupancy, the listing brokers involved, and the buyer's motivations.

Additional information in the comparable was sourced from public records.

Clear: 22'0"; Land: 4.95 AC; Stoltz Real Estate Partners acquired a 25 property, ~2.8M SF portfolio of industrial buildings in TN, NC, and GA from Dogwood Industrial Properties for $367,750,000 or $130 PSF.

Starwood Property Trust provided financing of $150,660,800 for the deal as well as Invesco Real Estate Finance Investments LP of $128,010,000. The loan through Invesco was also secured for acquisitions this year by Stoltz,  not affiliated with this comparable. See CoStar Comp ID 6740013 for an $18M acquisition in May and 6681941 for a $41M deal in March for additional info into the acquisitions.

The details of this transaction derived from public record information and historical tracking of the inventory. A contact involved in the sale noted the $367.75M pricing. No SF total has been noted by the parties.    Please note, pricing on 5 properties in GA and 2 in TN reflect allocated values. Public record instead notes $37.7M for 4200-4600 Highlands Pky and $12.5M for 6680-6690 Jones Ct in Georgia. 1416-1418 An

a: 2406 Tech Center Pkwy NE, Lawrenceville, GA 30043, USA
cmt:
Clear: 30'0"; Land: 43.54 AC; The property traded for $120,000,300 from Blackstone, to LBA Advisors out of Irvine California. 

It is a group of four buildings. Each sold on a separate parcel totalling up to a little over 80 acres.
7415 Emerald Dunes sold for $42.6 million for 233,280 sf
7433 Emerald Dunes sold for $20.1 million for 150,824 sf
7443 Emerald Dunes sold for $27.1 million for 109,704 sf
7455 Emerald Dunes sold for $30 million for 159,884 sf.

Proximity to the airport makes this well established business park a great location with airport clients such as Jet Blue and Thales.

Information obtained from public record.

a: 3641 Atlanta Industrial Dr NW, Atlanta, GA 30331, USA
buyer: BlusV II GA 3665 Atlanta Industria; Drive LLC
cmt:
Leased: 55.00; Clear: 26'0"; Land: 10.32 AC; Blackstone Real Estate Partners VIII, a fund managed by Blackstone Inc. sold this 415,153 three-buidling industrial park for $43,575,000 or $104.96 per square foot.

Based on available marketing with CoStar/Loopnet at the time of the sale; 3641 NW Atlanta Industrial (building 200) had 80,576 SF vacant at the time of the sale or was 55% leased. Buildings 100 and 300 were fully leased at the time of the sale producing a overall occupancy of  81%  for the industrial park.

Information for this comparable report was collected from public records and sources deemed reliable.

a: 6755 Shiloh Rd E, Alpharetta, GA 30005, USA
cmt:
Leased: 100.00; Clear: 24'0"; Land: 5.77 AC; Prologis has sold three shallow-bay industrial properties to GID for an $26,600,000 or $157 PSF. 

The subject properties were 97% occupied with average rent of $9.48 PSF (28% below market rent of $12.12 PSF) and a 2.9 year WALT, delivering a 5.85% in-place CAP rate. Two were in Royal Atlanta Industrial Park with one in Tucker GA. 

The details of this transaction were confirmed with sources deemed reliable.

a: 5123 S Royal Atlanta Dr, Tucker, GA 30084, USA

Leased: 100.00; Clear: 22'0"; Land: 4.56 AC; ATCAP Partners acquired 12 buildings within Gwinnett Park from TPG affiliate Dogwood Industrial totaling 753,737 Sf for $100,770,000 or $134 PSF. 

Originally under contract for $102.6M, the property was repriced based on treasury movement. The properties were built in phases between 1973-86 and 5 roofs were in the hold. They feature 18-22 Ft clear heights, 90-109 foot truck courts and 29% of the space is finished for offices. The portfolio was 94% leased between 36 tenants with a 2.6 year WALT, delivering an in-place 4.83% CAP or $4,867,191 in net operating income. Most properties were fully occupied, besides 4295 International Blvd at 35% and 4487 Park Dr at 93.34%. The buyer secured financing through Empower Investments. The transaction presented a 46% mark to market opporunity with no details into a pro-forma disclosed. 

The details of this transaction were confirmed with sources deemed reliable. Please note, pricing on individual properties reflected in CoStar were allocated bas

a: 4680 Lewis Rd, Stone Mountain, GA 30083, USA
seller: UB II New Wincup GA LLC
cmt:
Leased: 100.00; Land: 3.92 AC; Faropoint,  through its new sale leaseback fund acquired this 220,380 SF New Wincup Portfolio from Brennan Investment Group for $16,500,000 or $75 PSF. 

The new buyers fund seems indicative of a sale leaseback, although no ties for Wincup, or its parent Atar Capital, was found in documentation. No loan recorded with the deal. 

The details of this transaction derived from public record details and the attached press release.

Clear: 36'0"; Land: 27.70 AC; Longpoint Realty Partners through its Fund II acquired the subject 327,825 SF logistics center from the developer, TPA Group, for $39,339,000 or $120 PSF in an all cash deal. 

This was a forward purchase agreement with a contract signed in December 2021 before ground break. It was recently issued its certificate of occupancy and sold vacant while still in lease-up. The sales price reflects a 7.08% pro-forma CAP rate if current market rates of around $8.50 NNN are achieved. 

The details of this transaction derived from public record and were confirmed with sources deemed reliable.

a: 2223 Thornton Rd, Lithia Springs, GA 30122, USA
cmt:
Clear: 32'0"; Land: 6.25 AC; Huntington Industrial Partners sold the 85,133 square foot building to Sunco Lighting for $13,600,000 or $159.75 per square foot. The building was 100% vacant at the time of the sale.
The listing was on the market for 3 months with no initial asking price.
The information in the comparable has been confirmed by the listing and buyer brokers.

Leased: 93.76; Clear: 21'0"; Land: 5.87 AC; A JV between Atlanta Property Group and TPG Angelo Gordon acquired this 99,762 SF industrial building from Eugenia Investments for $10,600,000 or $106 PSF. 

The multi-tenant building was about 93% occupied at the time of sale delivering an undisclosed CAP rate. It features 13 dock doors and 21 ft clear height. The buyer secured financing through American United Life Insurance valued at $6,360,000. 

The details of this transaction were confirmed with sources deemed reliable.

a: 1110 Allgood Industrial Ct NE, Marietta, GA 30066, USA
cmt:
Leased: 100.00; Land: 6.07 AC; Kohlberg Kravis Roberts &amp; Co. L.P. sold an undisclosed number of industrial properties in a portfolio to High Street Logistics Properties. The industrial portfolio features warehouse and distribution properties in Georgia, Pennsylvania, and Texas. 

The known portion of the portfolio features:

Leased: 100.00; Clear: 18'0"; Land: 6.50 AC; Estes Express Lines has acquired 26 locations totaling 977,217 square feet in a $248,720,505 bankruptcy sale from Yellow Corporation (YRC Inc). 

Per a new article, States was interested in the locations as “We really focused on the Canada border,” Estes said. “Focused on the gateways, because we’ve seen a lot of growth from Yellow’s exodus there and believe that we have a lot more opportunity to grow into Canada.”

Yellow filed for bankruptcy early August, a week after it shut down all operations. It was the largest bankruptcy filing for a trucking company in U.S. history, as the company employed more than 30,000 people. The company was in operation for 99 years.

This is the second highest sale in the Yellow bankruptcy sale. XPO acquired 26 locations for $870,000,000. (comp 6605197).
